_meta.description: English seed templates per intent. Placeholders: {service} {symptom} {time} {first} {last} {street_num} {street} {suffix} {job_ref}. Expanded by scripts/data-pipeline/generate-utterances.ts.

create_appointment: Can I get someone out to look at my {service} {time}?
I'd like to schedule a visit, my {service} {symptom}.
Can you send a tech {time} for my {service}?
I need to book an appointment for the {service}.
Set me up for {time} if you can, it's the {service}.
We need somebody to come out about the {service}, {time} works.
How soon can you get here? The {service} {symptom}.
reschedule_appointment: I need to move my appointment to {time}.
Can we push my visit to {time} instead?
Something came up, can I reschedule for {time}?
I have to change my appointment, {time} would be better.
Can you bump my appointment back to {time}?
cancel_appointment: I need to cancel my appointment for {time}.
Go ahead and cancel the visit, something came up.
I have to cancel the {service} appointment.
Can you cancel my appointment? I won't be home.
I don't need the tech to come anymore, cancel it.
confirm_appointment: Just calling to confirm you're still coming {time}.
Are we still on for the appointment {time}?
I wanted to confirm my {service} appointment.
Confirming the visit — you're coming out, right?
callback: Can someone call me back when you get a chance?
I'd like a callback about my {service}.
Have somebody call me back {time}, please.
Nobody's available now? Just call me back later.
Can you have a person call me back about a quote?
emergency_dispatch: My basement is flooding, water is everywhere, I can't shut it off!
There's water gushing out of the {service}, I need someone right now!
I smell gas in the house, what do I do?
Sewage is backing up into the tub, it's an emergency.
A pipe burst and water is pouring out, please hurry!
We have no heat and it's freezing, the {service} is completely dead.
The carbon monoxide alarm is going off, the {service} won't shut off.
create_customer: Hi, I'm a new customer, I'd like to set up service.
I've never used you before, can I get on the books?
I'm new — my name is {first} {last}, I need some work done.
First time calling, I need to set up an account.
I got your number from a neighbor, I'd like to become a customer.
update_customer: I moved, can you update my address on my account?
My phone number changed, can you update it?
Please update my email on file.
I've got a new number, update it for me — same account, {first} {last}.
Can you change the address you have for me to {street_num} {street} {suffix}?
add_service_location: I want to add my rental property to my account too.
Can you add a second address for me? It's {street_num} {street} {suffix}.
Add my mom's house to my account, same billing.
I've got another property I need on the account.
Put my shop address on the account as a second location.
convert_lead: Yeah let's do it, go ahead and set me up.
Okay, I'm ready to move forward.
Let's go ahead with the work you quoted.
I talked it over, let's book it.
create_job: My {service} {symptom} and I need that fixed.
I've got a problem with the {service}, it {symptom}.
Can you open a job for me? The {service} {symptom}.
draft_estimate: Can you send me an estimate for replacing the {service}?
How about a written quote for the {service} work?
I'd like an estimate before I commit, for the {service}.
Can I get a quote in writing for fixing the {service}?
Send me an estimate for the {service}, please.
send_estimate: Can you text me that estimate you mentioned?
Email me the estimate when you can.
Can you resend the estimate? I lost it.
Go ahead and send over that quote.
Forward me the estimate for the {service}.
draft_invoice: Can I get an invoice for the work you did last week?
I need an invoice for the {service} repair.
Can you send me a bill for the job?
I need a receipt — can you invoice me?
Send me the invoice for the {job_ref}.
record_payment: I want to pay my bill, can I give you a card?
I'd like to pay the balance over the phone.
Can I make a payment on my account?
Let me pay what I owe, I've got a check.
I'm calling to pay my invoice.
log_expense: Log forty dollars of fittings to {job_ref}.
Put twenty bucks of materials on {job_ref}.
Charge the parts to {job_ref}, about sixty dollars.
Log the supply run, thirty five dollars, to {job_ref}.
log_time_entry: Put me down for two hours on {job_ref}.
Log an hour and a half of labor on {job_ref}.
Clock me for two hours on {job_ref}.
I spent about ninety minutes on {job_ref}, log it.
add_note: Add a note that the gate code is four two nine one.
Put a note on the account — dog in the backyard.
Note that the customer prefers afternoon visits.
Add a note to {job_ref}: parts are on backorder.
Leave a note that the water is shut off at the street.
notify_delay: Let the ten o'clock know I'm running about twenty minutes behind.
Tell {job_ref} I'm going to be late.
Let the customer know I'm stuck in traffic, fifteen minutes out.
Notify my next appointment that I'm running behind.
Tell them I'll be there in half an hour, not on time.
request_feedback: Ask the customer for a review on that last job.
Have them send a review link to the customer.
mark_lead_lost: Mark the {last} lead as lost, they went with someone else.
Close out that lead, they're not moving forward.
The prospect passed, mark the lead lost.
Set that lead to lost, no response.
They decided not to do it, mark it lost.
lookup_appointments: When is my next appointment again?
Do I have anything scheduled this week?
Can you remind me when the tech is coming?
What's on my schedule with you?
lookup_jobs: What's the status on the job you opened for me?
Where are we at on the {service} repair?
Did the tech finish the job?
Any update on my service request?
lookup_invoices: How much do I owe you right now?
Do I have any open invoices?
Did my last payment go through?
What do I still owe on the {service} job?
lookup_estimates: Did that estimate ever go out to me?
What was the price on the estimate you sent?
Can you tell me what the estimate covered?
What's the total on my estimate?
lookup_account_summary: Can you tell me what's on my account?
Give me a rundown of my account.
What do you have on file for me?
Can you summarize my account real quick?
What's my account look like right now?
lookup_customer: Do you have me in the system? It's under my wife's name.
Am I in your records? Last name {last}.
Can you look me up? I think it's under my old number.
Do you have my address on file?
Check if I'm a customer already, {first} {last}.
pricing_question: Roughly how much do you charge to snake a main line?
What's it run to replace a {service}?
How much for a service call?
Ballpark, what would the {service} repair cost?
What do you charge to come out and look at the {service}?
hours_location: Do you guys cover the east side of town?
Do you service my area? I'm out past the highway.
service_availability: Do you all work on tankless water heaters?
Is the {service} something you guys do?
Do you do commercial work or just residential?
complaint: Your guy was supposed to come yesterday and nobody showed up.
The {service} you fixed is broken again already.
I waited all day and no one came.
The tech left a mess and the {service} still {symptom}.
This is the third time I've called about the same problem.
payment_dispute: I'm looking at this bill and there's no way it should be this much.
You charged me twice for the same job.
This invoice doesn't match what I was quoted.
I never agreed to this charge on the {service}.
There's a mistake on my bill, I want it fixed.
dnc_optout: Take me off your list and don't call me again.
I want to be on your do-not-call list.
Quit texting me, I'm not interested.
Remove my number from your system.
greeting: Hi there, how's it going today?
Hello? Can you hear me okay?
agent_handoff_request: Can I just talk to an actual person please?
Is there a human I can speak to?
I'd rather talk to a real person.
Put me through to someone, please.
Can you transfer me to a person?
